item.title: Tighten enforce_groom_limits and protect_items_from_groom
item.intent: Groom protection and limit enforcement logic is tighter and less verbose. Combined size drops from 149 to ~100 lines while preserving all safety guarantees.
item.description: enforce_groom_limits (76 lines) and protect_items_from_groom (73 lines) are correct but verbose. The protection function does per-field comparison with a 6-field jq check then a separate merge — this can be a single jq expression. The limits function iterates items one-by-one in bash when a single jq filter would suffice.
steps: Rewrite protect_items_from_groom to use a single jq pass: for each pre-existing ID, replace the live item with the original but carry over safe fields
Rewrite enforce_groom_limits to use jq to filter new items by priority rank and cap count, then remove excess in one pass
Remove per-item bash loops where jq can do the work
Keep all log_warning output for transparency
Verify: groom with a snapshot produces identical protection behavior
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-existing items are never modified by groomer (title, intent, desc, steps, AC preserved)
New items exceeding max count or below min priority are removed
